qa_pairs.question: What is a typical Slurm script configuration for submitting an Ansys Mechanical APDL Distributed Memory Parallel (DMP) job using CPUs on an Alliance cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: To submit a CPU-based Distributed Memory Parallel (DMP) job for Ansys Mechanical APDL, a Slurm script generally includes specifying your account, a time limit, and memory per CPU (e.g., `#SBATCH --mem-per-cpu=4G`). You need to load the `StdEnv/2023` and `ansys/2024R1.03` modules (or newer versions). The `mapdl` command utilizes the `-dis` option for distributed parallel processing and selects the MPI implementation based on the cluster: `intelmpi` for Beluga or `openmpi` for other clusters. For the Cedar cluster, an `LD_LIBRARY_PATH` adjustment is required to handle GCC library dependencies. An example command would be `mapdl -dis -mpi [intelmpi|openmpi] -b nolist -np $SLURM_NTASKS -dir outdir-$SLURM_JOBID -i YOURAPDLFILE.inp`.

qa_pairs.question: How can one configure a Slurm script to run an Ansys Mechanical APDL Shared Memory Parallel (SMP) job with GPU acceleration on an Alliance cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: To run a Shared Memory Parallel (SMP) Ansys Mechanical APDL job with GPU acceleration, your Slurm script should specify your account, a time limit, total memory (e.g., `#SBATCH --mem=32G`), and request a GPU resource using `#SBATCH --gpus-per-node=1` (or specify the GPU type and quantity if needed). You must load `StdEnv/2023` and `ansys/2024R1.03` (or newer modules). It is crucial to export `ANSGPU_PRINTDEVICES=1` before execution. The `mapdl` command should include `-smp`, `-acc nvidia`, and `-na $SLURM_GPUS_ON_NODE` to enable GPU usage. For the Cedar cluster, a `LD_LIBRARY_PATH` adjustment may also be necessary. A sample execution command is `mapdl -smp -acc nvidia -na $SLURM_GPUS_ON_NODE -b nolist -np $SLURM_NTASKS -dir outdir-$SLURM_JOBID -i YOURAPDLFILE.inp`.

qa_pairs.question: What are the essential components of a Slurm script for running an Ansys Mechanical APDL Distributed Memory Parallel (DMP) job with GPU support on an Alliance cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: For a GPU-accelerated Distributed Memory Parallel (DMP) Ansys Mechanical APDL job, the Slurm script must specify your account, a time limit, memory per CPU (e.g., `#SBATCH --mem-per-cpu=4G`), and the number of GPUs per node (e.g., `#SBATCH --gpus-per-node=1`). You are required to load the `StdEnv/2023` and `ansys/2024R1.03` modules (or newer versions) and export `ANSGPU_PRINTDEVICES=1`. The `mapdl` command should utilize `-dis` for distributed parallel, `-acc nvidia`, `-na $SLURM_GPUS_ON_NODE`, and the appropriate MPI implementation: `intelmpi` for Beluga or `openmpi` for other clusters. Similar to other Ansys Mechanical jobs, an `LD_LIBRARY_PATH` adjustment might be needed for Cedar clusters. A sample command is `mapdl -dis -acc nvidia -na $SLURM_GPUS_ON_NODE -mpi [intelmpi|openmpi] -b nolist -np $SLURM_NTASKS -dir outdir-$SLURM_JOBID -i YOURAPDLFILE.inp`.
